reviews.review_description:
This app lets you test out furniture arrangements virtually, BUT has very limited models.

You have basic shapes like a square, circle, cube, or sphere, and you have very crude furniture limited to simple chairs, bookcases, tables, and electronics.

Would rate better if I could group basic shapes together so I can virtually represent less simple or custom furniture.

"This app is amazing! I can plan a whole room design!! What a life saver. Now that I'm done can I save this?"

No.

Can I share it with myself or other people.

Nah.

Can I export a still or actual 3d cad.

Nope?

Why not?

Because we don't actually like you and would never actually make something that might actually help you in any way shape of form.

This app is amazing. It’s insanely creative & satisfying to make objects and place them in your world. I just wish you could import the creations into Horizon Worlds because it is so intuitive, unlike the creation suite in in aforementioned app. 
Want to see what your living room will look like w/ 4 arcade machines in it? Done. Want to make just about anything as a 3-D vector? Done. Want to see what space you have for the coffee table the significant other wants? Done. Have a creative itch? This is the app for you. If there were more play features it would be an easy 5stars. Keep this kind of momentum up!
This was actually a very useful app until it completely stopped working. I tried restarting the app multiple times, restarting my headset, and even uninstalled the app twice. Still no luck.
Doesn't work

Just what I needed in planning additions to my house. The controls are pretty infuriating, the way you use the thumb sticks to turn objects and select what mode you’re in makes no sense. The way the measurements are displayed in imperial but entering the measurements use decimals (2ft 3in = 2.25, 43.5in = 3.625). Your cursor snapping to inputs only work well when you’re trying to manipulate large objects up close otherwise it always snaps to the thing you don’t want.

I’m currently extending my house and this has been really useful to show the kids some room layout options - and it’s got them really excited and able to visualise how their rooms (currently un-plastered shells) will look when we’re finished.

The controls can be a bit frustrating but manageable when you get used to them.

Unless I’m being stupid I can’t see an option to save multiple  room layouts - when I want to set up a second room it forgets the first one. 

This could be user error but if the feature exists it’s not intuitive
Stop force installing this useless stuff on my device. If I wanted it I would download it myself.
Pre downloaded games are made for a reason, mixed reality games may also use this application meaning to use your current games or items this PRE-DOWNLOADED app may need to be active
Stop taking storage from me!

I was shopping around for a rug for my home but was unsure of the size to get. This tool helped me pick out the correct size I needed for the space. Very helpful in visualizing the size of things even if you don't have them there with you.

I can see the potential in this app though it needs a lot of polish and features to be added like more furniture objects like indoor plants fireplaces consoles etc. me and my partner are renovating our house so for me this tool is great to visualise the outcome better, but it would be amazing if you could add different style staircases since we are putting a spiral staircase in it would be amazing to see 

4.5/10
Meta does not allow these useless apps to be deleted👎🏼
Sooooii good i got to pick the one I like
It’s great to see a utility app like this, right now it’s not amazing. But the basic stuff works well. Few glitched and lack of options, but definitely heading in the right direction.

I'm just buying a new couch, so this app proved a great help in visualising how it will look inside my living room.

I do wish we'd be able to choose the colour for MR objects though. Right now bright blue is too dominant and makes MR furniture look unnatural compared to the real room.

It's a start in the right direction though. Already useful, but with more options it might become a must for decorating our homes.

I recently moved into a new property and this has been really useful for planning how furniture might fit into the rooms. For instance, my partner wanted to buy a particular cabinet from Marketplace and, using the dimensions, I was able to mock it up and show that it wouldn't be suitable for the space.

An unexpected use case is that I have been able to plot the electrics in adjacent rooms, allowing me to drill into walls, knowing that I'm not going to hit the wiring. It's like having x-ray vision!

Overall, this app has been incredibly useful to me and I'm looking forward to using it more in future.

Possible improvements: 

- I would like the ability to group shapes into a single movable object

- I'd prefer to be able to increase the size of objects directionally, rather than the object expanding from the center. This would make it easier to use shapes in combination.
Poor UI, limited use and often crashes. Another "alpha" release by Meta.
So far it's useful for seeing what glowing neon furniture would look like in your space, and roughly measuring things. The level seems to work perfectly though.
Now link to ikea models pls

Perhaps better known as a ‘Killer App’.  This is why I would buy a Quest.  This is why Quest could be “sticky” for me.  Why I would save money to by Meta’s next ambient device — well done. The utility, for even an initial limited release app, is off the charts.  The app sets UI standards/expectations for styling, design and CAD apps.  Meta Layout informs developers of what is possible.  Layout is my favorite 1st-Party app for Quest of all time.  I could see using this to teach related math concepts such as area, units, etc.
I'm looking forward to useful mixed reality apps and this app has potential, but it doesn't seem to be much more than a bare bones tech demo forced into my library.
I was playing a game in a big area, and almost hit a wall, I only knew the wall was there because this helped me
I will rarely use it but its fun to mess around with it
Good app, lots of potential if you could add plug sockets and that would be good

Once we’re able to choose from presets designs and diy modes for the furniture and room’s overall appearance and other objects highlighted in the app, I’ll be changing my review to 5 stars, because that’s what I see happening here in the near future… and if not, then you’re wasting a decent idea. Did most other people think you’d be able to choose the way your furniture or room looks virtually? Just me? Either way the app is already fantastic, just waiting for those customization options you guys! I’ll be lurking in the meantime lol.

Keeping it short, the first updates I would work on after setting up a single room:
- get rid off the "snap" that moves the object in space every time you want to interact with it, it's counter productive to mess your object's position every time you want to fix it
- align/snap to other objects for position and rotation
- "lock" transform before releasing the trigger, the app picks up the controller movement when you release the trigger which makes it virtually impossible to release the transform where you want it
- incremental transforms/controlled sensitivity
- save/open
- import sketchfab objects

Right now it's pretty annoying to use ;) but so close to be a very impressive and useful tool. Quest  needs apps like this one.

The precision has actually beaten my expectations and the app is pretty easy to use. It is a bit late and I found that the dots for measuring are a bit too big (especially with smaller items), but overall pretty good.

This app is clearly in the early days if you want basic room layout for furniture this will do it! A larger selection of objects would work wonders. I see where this is going imagine being able to choose real furniture etc from your retailer and have it delivered directly from the app that would be the most amazing thing!!! I would pay for that app! I will change my review accordingly to updates.
Massive potential. Please keep updating it.

Tried using this app to map and plan a room for a house move. Spent well over an hour building, calibrating and fine tuning the area. Initially looked fantastic, but when removing headset to charge it, all the items moved around and the calibration was thrown out. After spending another 30 mins fixing it, I then realised that there is no way to save the design, so next time I loaded it up to show the family, the AR furnishings all had disappeared... Over an hour and half of effort down the drain. Please improve on this as it has potential but just is a buggy mess.

This software is very precise and convenient. I can use it to determine the size and location of my room decoration. However, currently there are not enough features and models, and we hope to add more features and models

reviews.review_title: Off to a good start, still a ways to go
reviews.review_description: The basic usefulness of this app is undeniable. You can move the preset objects around, take measurements of existing items, and visualize a new room layout, shift it all around, and try again. That is, a layout limited by a small number of preset items. When this app allows for the input or creation of custom or more realistic items, it truly will be a 'killer app' for aspiring interior decorators.
